regions.priceFilter: PASS — sub-$800k still available in Armadale ($605k), Midland ($636k), Wellard ($690k), Mandurah ($575k)
regions.note: Perth +26% annual = strongest capital. 0.6% vacancy, structural supply shortage. Midland just got new train station Feb 2026. WA defence + mining drives jobs. WINDOW CLOSING — affordable suburbs being priced out monthly.
regions.keyRisk: Cycle risk — 26% growth may moderate. Some outer suburbs have crime concerns (Armadale).

regions.note: Townsville +15.6% YTD. 0.6% vacancy + 9.1% rental growth. Defence + CopperString HVDC + mining base. Propertyology ranks #1 for capital growth. Population +30% to 264k by 2046. Cash-flow positive territory.
regions.keyRisk: Second QLD property = extra ~$500-1500/yr land tax. Climate (cyclone) considerations.
analystForecasts: Propertyology: BEST capital growth in AU

regions.note: Brisbane +19.7% annual, 0.8% vacancy. Cross River Rail + 2032 Olympics infrastructure. Ripley fast-growing corridor. Logan affordability draws investors fleeing Brisbane prices.
regions.keyRisk: Logan parts have crime concerns. Ipswich greenfield = supply risk.

regions.priceFilter: PASS — Para Hills $520k, Salisbury $615k, Ingle Farm $700k, Elizabeth North $510k
regions.note: Adelaide +12.2% annual. 0.5% vacancy = tightest in nation. 75% clearance rate = strongest capital. AUKUS submarine base = decades of defence jobs. Affordable entry for new investors.
regions.keyRisk: Some northern suburbs have crime/public housing concentration (Davoren Park, Salisbury Downs).
analystForecasts: Elizabeth North highest yields in city

regions.note: Outer Adelaide cheaper entry but Two Wells 43k new dwellings = HIGH supply risk. Established areas safer.
regions.keyRisk: MASSIVE supply risk in outer ring — Two Wells/Roseworthy/Riverlea releases.
analystForecasts: Adelaide growth strong but outer rings face dilution

regions.priceFilter: PASS — Hampton Park $680k, Cranbourne West $695k, Narre Warren $720k
regions.note: Melbourne only +2% annual = WORST capital. But growth corridors Wyndham/Cardinia/Casey attract young families. Supply rising. VIC investor surcharge ~1.5% extra stamp duty.
regions.keyRisk: VIC +1.5% stamp duty surcharge + lowest growth. Land tax changes. Melbourne -0.9% Q1.

regions.note: Regional VIC contender but VIC tax disadvantages. Lifestyle/commuter market. Lower growth than QLD/WA/SA.
regions.keyRisk: VIC tax regime, slower growth, oversupply in some new estates.

regions.note: Diversified inland QLD economy. Wellcamp airport, university, defence. Steady growth, less cyclical than coast.
regions.keyRisk: Slower growth than coastal QLD. Inland positioning.

regions.priceFilter: BORDERLINE — Pimpama $700k, Coomera $680k. Most GC over budget.
regions.note: Strong tourism + lifestyle. Coomera/Pimpama outer rings under budget. Climate considerations.
regions.keyRisk: Most suburbs above $800k budget. Climate (flood/storm).

regions.note: Affordable WA entry. Train to Perth. Lifestyle + retirees + workers. Coastal premium.
regions.keyRisk: Distance from Perth CBD. Retiree-skewed demographic.

regions.note: Regional WA. Mining/agriculture. High yield potential but thinner market.
regions.keyRisk: Single-industry exposure. Thin liquidity.

regions.note: Second-city NSW. Hunter Valley + port + university. Some outer suburbs under budget.
regions.keyRisk: Most suburbs over $800k. NSW slower than WA/QLD.

regions.note: Sydney commuter belt. Lifestyle premium. Most suburbs over budget.
regions.keyRisk: Over budget. Sydney market influence.

regions.note: Tropical north QLD. Tourism + ag + mining. Climate considerations. High yield potential.
regions.keyRisk: Climate (cyclone/flood). Tourism-dependent. Smaller market.

regions.note: Resources-dependent. High yield 6-7%. Volatile with commodity cycle.
regions.keyRisk: Mining concentration risk. Boom/bust history.

regions.note: Post-cycle. Slowed considerably after 2020-22 boom. Some opportunity for cash-flow plays.
regions.keyRisk: Late-cycle market. Smaller economy. Limited liquidity.

regions.note: Affordable but thin market. Late cycle.
regions.keyRisk: Thin market, liquidity risk, late cycle.

regions.note: Defence base + new energy projects. Volatile history. Recent recovery momentum.
regions.keyRisk: Climate. Boom/bust history. Single-industry exposure.

regions.note: Stable government jobs. Limited stock under $800k. Lower growth.
regions.keyRisk: Over budget for houses. Government policy exposure.

regions.note: Brisbane growth ring. Olympics infrastructure benefit. Train lines extending. Murrumba Downs (your existing property) area.
regions.keyRisk: Second QLD property = land tax. Some flood-prone pockets.

summary: After 12 May 2026 negative gearing changes, regional priorities shift. STRONG picks remain Perth mid/outer, Townsville, Logan/Ipswich — all sub-$800k with structural growth drivers. Adelaide north strong but watch supply risk in outer ring. Melbourne/VIC weakened by tax surcharge + lowest growth. Most NSW over budget. CRITICAL: With negative gearing dead for new established purchases, all picks need to be CASH FLOW POSITIVE from day one OR be new builds. Townsville (0.6% vacancy + 9.1% rent growth) leads cash-flow viability. Perth strongest growth but reaching cycle peak.
avoids: NSW — Sydney inner/middle ($1.76M median, well over budget)
VIC — Melbourne inner (lowest growth + 1.5% surcharge + tax disadvantages)
Outer Adelaide greenfield (Two Wells 43k new dwellings = supply risk)
